result:
Everything is wired up. Here's what was implemented:

**Minimap overlay for the Game of Life explorer:**

- **Quarter-block rendering** — the full 400x200 grid is downsampled into a compact thumbnail using Unicode quadrant characters (2x2 subpixels per character), matching the existing zoom-4x technique
- **Viewport rectangle** — a bright yellow border shows exactly where you're looking in the grid, making pan navigation intuitive
- **Adaptive sizing** — minimap scales to 1/4 terminal width and 1/3 usable height, capped at 50x25 chars, with a minimum threshold below which it hides
- **Dark background overlay** — dark blue-gray background (`rgb(20,20,30)`) with box-drawing border so it stands out from grid content beneath it
- **Auto-show when zoomed** — appears automatically at zoom 2x/4x, hidden at 1x (where you already see the full grid)
- **Toggle with `n`** — status bar shows `▣MAP` indicator when active; help text updated

The minimap positions itself in the bottom-right corner of the grid area, using ANSI cursor positioning to overlay on top of the rendered grid content.
